injected: pipeline/transform.py: the upstream orders.dropna(subset=["customer_id"]) call was removed, and the groupby(...) call was changed to dropna=False. Orders with no customer_id are no longer excluded -- they survive the join and land in the output as an explicit NaN-customer_id group.
intended_fix: Restore orders = orders.dropna(subset=["customer_id"]).copy() before the merge (or, equivalently, just flip groupby(..., dropna=False) back to dropna=True -- either change alone is sufficient to exclude the unattributable rows).
symptom_fix_to_watch_for: Don't fix this by loosening pipeline/schema.py's MONTHLY_REVENUE customer_id column to nullable=True -- schema.py is protected, and that just papers over the leak instead of excluding unattributable orders.
